children.text: to build or expand correctional facilities to increase the bed capacity for the confinement of persons convicted of a part 1 violent crime or adjudicated delinquent for an act which if committed by an adult, would be a part 1 violent crime;

to build or expand temporary or permanent correctional facilities, including facilities on military bases, prison barges, and boot camps, for the confinement of convicted nonviolent offenders and criminal aliens, for the purpose of freeing suitable existing prison space for the confinement of persons convicted of a part 1 violent crime;
to build or expand jails; and
to carry out any activity referred to in .section 10631(b) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), States may enter into regional compacts to carry out this part. Such compacts shall be treated as States under this part.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: 2 Requirement
children.text: To be recognized as a regional compact for eligibility for a grant under section 12103 or 12104 of this title, each member State must be eligible individually.

children.heading: Limitation on receipt of funds
children.label: 3 Limitation on receipt of funds
children.text: No State may receive a grant under this part both individually and as part of a compact.

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: c Applicability
children.text: Notwithstanding the eligibility requirements of , a State that certifies to the Attorney General that, as of , such State has enacted legislation in reliance on this part, as enacted on , and would in fact qualify under those provisions, shall be eligible to receive a grant for fiscal year 1996 as though such State qualifies under .section 12104 of this titlesection 12104 of this titleApril 26, 1996September 13, 1994
